a_image.alt: Windows 95 UI style mood board — Operating System · 1995–2001

sections.a_eyebrow: Retro · Nostalgic · Flat Design · Bold Colors · Professional
sections.a_description: The unmistakable silver-gray window chrome, beveled 3D borders, and navy blue title bars of Microsoft's most iconic operating system — Windows 95 UI brings the desktop revolution to the web. Every element carries the raised-button, sunken-panel depth system that defined a generation of computing, where every click produced a satisfying visual press and every window felt like a tangible object on your screen.

Windows 95 styling appears on retro computing fan sites, nostalgic personal homepages, and creative projects that want to evoke the dawn of mainstream personal computing. You'll find it on developer side projects, internet art installations, and indie apps that use the familiar gray-and-blue palette as either genuine homage or playful irony.

a_image_cover.alt: Windows 95 UI style use case illustration
Windows 95 UI style use case illustration
Windows 95 UI style use case illustration

sections.a_description: Faithful recreation of the Windows 95/98 operating system aesthetic. Silver-gray background (#c0c0c0) — the classic window chrome color. The .inner panel is styled as a raised window with the signature Windows beveled border: 2px outset border using white (#ffffff) top-left highlight and dark gray (#808080) bottom-right shadow, plus a 1px #dfdfdf outer edge. Inside the panel, a blue title-bar stripe (#000080 navy) at the top via pseudo-element. Text is pure black (#000000) on gray. Zero border-radius anywhere — Windows 95 had no rounded corners. Set in IBM Plex Sans — a system-UI-flavored sans-serif that channels the Tahoma/MS Sans Serif energy of the Windows 95 era while remaining a proper web font. Heading at weight 700, 28px, line-height 1.2. Eyebrow at weight 600, 11px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, navy (#000080). Body at weight 400, 14px, line-height 1.6, black. Buttons replicate the classic Windows raised-button look: #c0c0c0 background with 2px outset border (white top-left, #808080 bottom-right). On hover/:active, the border flips to inset (pressed state). Primary button: navy background (#000080), white text, outset border. Secondary: gray background, black text, outset border. Alternatives: Inter (neutral system font feel), Segoe UI (modern Windows), Roboto (Android system font).
